data.title: So-Young announces extension of plan to implement ADS ratio change
data.description:
• So-Young International (NASDAQ:SY) on Friday announced that it is amending the effective date for the previously announced plan for an ADS ratio change.

  • The company had previously announced plans to change the ratio of its American depositary shares to its Class A ordinary shares, from thirteen ADSs representing ten Class A ordinary shares to one ADS representing fifteen Class A ordinary shares. 

  • Following further consideration, the Company has decided to take additional time to finalize preparations for the ADS ratio change. An updated timeline will be announced once it becomes available.

data.title: KKR updates on quarter-to-date monetization activity
data.description:
KKR (NYSE:KKR) said on Friday that its asset management unit earned realized performance income and investment income of more than $475M quarter-to-date through June 20.

The amount includes performance income that is expected to be crystallized at June 30.

During Q1, the company had reported total monetization activity of $566M (+40% year-over-year). On March 24, KKR had reported realized performance income and investment income of over $475M for the period from January 1 to March 24.

For the latest update, the monetization activity quarter-to-date in the second quarter is made up of ~75% realized performance income and about 25% realized investment income.

data.title: Cassiar Gold announces offering of up to C$5M non-brokered private placement of common shares
Nvidia, Foxconn mull deployment of humanoid robots at Houston AI server manufacturing facility - report
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Foxconn Technology (OTCPK:FXCOF) are in discussions to deploy humanoid robots at a new Foxconn facility in Houston that will make Nvidia AI servers, Reuters reported citing people with knowledge of the matter.

This would be the first time that an Nvidia product will be made with the help of humanoid robots and would be Foxconn's first AI server factory to use them on a production line, the report added.

Foxconn and Nvidia did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Foxconn, also known as Hon Hai Precision (OTCPK:HNHAF) (OTCPK:HNHPF), is developing its own humanoid robots with Nvidia and has also trialed humanoids made by China's UBTech. However, it was not clear what type of humanoid robots are being planned to be used in the Houston facility, what they will look like or how many will be deployed initially, according to the report.

The two companies are thinking of deploying the humanoid robots at work by the first quarter of next year when Foxconn's new Houston facility will start production of Nvidia's GB300 AI servers, the report noted.

It is not clear what the robots will be doing exactly at the facility. Foxconn has been training them to pick and place things, insert cables and carry out assembly work, the report added citing a company presentation in May.

The Houston facility was suitable to deploy humanoid robots because it will be new and has more area than other existing AI server production sites, the report noted.

At an industry event last month in Taipei, an executive at Foxconn Industrial Internet, noted that the company plans to showcase two versions of humanoid robots at the company's annual technology event in November.

One will have legs, while the other will use a wheeled autonomous mobile robot, or AMR, base, which would cost less than the version with legs, he said, as per the report.

In April, Nvidia said it planned to build AI supercomputer manufacturing factories in Texas, collaborating with Foxconn in Houston and Wistron in Dallas. Both facilities are expected to ramp up manufacturing within 12 to 15 months, the report added.

Earlier this week, Swedish industrial software and sensor company Hexagon launched a humanoid robot called Aeon, which is built using Nvidia's full robotics stack.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Darden Restaurants issues 2026 outlook, hikes dividend after Q4 beat
Uber expands AI data platform to train smart agents
data.description:
• Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) is expanding its AI data services arm, Uber AI Solutions, Uber AI Solutions, by offering its technology platform to support AI labs and businesses globally.
  • The new offerings include customized data solutions for building smarter AI models and agents, global digital task networks, and tools to help companies build and test AI models more efficiently.
  • “We’re bringing together Uber’s platform, people, and AI systems to help other organizations build smarter AI more quickly,” Megha Yethadka, head of Uber AI Solutions, said in a statement.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Accenture in charts: FQ3 revenue rises Y/Y and Q/Q across all industry groups
Emeren to go private in $2/ADS deal
data.description:
Emeren (NYSE:SOL) +6.7% pre-market Friday after saying it agreed to be taken private by Shurya Vitra Ltd. at US$0.20 per ordinary share, or US$2.00 per American Depositary Share; each ADS represents 10 shares.

Emeren said Chairman Himanshu Shah has committed to making an equity contribution to the buyer solely for the purpose of funding the merger consideration.

As of March 31, Emeren (NYSE:SOL) owned and operated IPP assets comprising ~293 MW of solar PV and 61 MWh of storage, with projects under development in Europe, the U.S. and China, according to its latest financial report.

data.title: GXO names Patrick Kelleher as CEO
data.description:
• GXO Logistics (NYSE:GXO) Friday announced the appointment of Patrick Kelleher as its new chief executive officer, effective August 19, 2025.

  • Kelleher brings 33 years of global supply chain experience, strategic leadership, and operational excellence to GXO, having held senior executive roles at DHL Supply Chain, a division of Deutsche Post DHL Group.

  • Most recently, he served as CEO, North America, where he oversaw significant growth and operational improvements across the business.

  • Kelleher will be based at GXO’s (NYSE:GXO) global headquarters in Greenwich, Connecticut. He will succeed Malcolm Wilson, who is retiring after a successful tenure leading GXO’s global growth strategy.

data.title: Accenture stock dips as new bookings fall 6% in Q3
data.description:
Accenture (NYSE:ACN) shares dipped nearly 4% premarket on Friday after the professional services company reported its fiscal third quarter results, highlighting an ongoing decline in new bookings.

The Irish company reported revenue of $17.7B for the quarter ended May 31 that beat analysts estimates by $380M. However, new bookings of $19.7B fell of 6% in U.S. dollars and 7% in local currency.

Generative AI new bookings amounted to $1.5B.

The company is facing challenges from a sluggish U.S. federal contracting landscape, as the Trump administration has slowed new awards and reduced existing agreements in an effort to curb federal spending.

GAAP earnings per share came at $3.49 that also topped Street estimates.

The firm updated its fiscal 2025 outlook, now expecting full-year revenue growth to be 6% to 7% in local currency, compared to +5%-+7% previously, and diluted earnings per share of $12.77 to $12.89 vs. $12.55 to $12.79 previously.

Accenture (NYSE:ACN) also lifted its free cash flow outlook to be in the range of $9B to $9.7B.

Related stocks: Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTSH), EPAM Systems (EPAM) and Infosys (INFY).
